title: Topology Declarations, Policies, and Migration · Go Semantics Case: Topology Owner Contract
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must make declaration equivalence, policy ownership, precondition errors, and rollback explicit in Go configuration; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the topology owner contract increment in a versioned Go topology ownership manifest. Learners progress through…
objectives: Assign ownership for exchanges, queues, bindings, policies, migrations, rollback, and deletion across application and platform teams; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Recover connection, channels, topology, confirm state, prefetch, consumers, and publisher admission in generation order while rejecting stale notifications and deliveries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Topology Owner Contract · Theory Topology Owner Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 6535 during canary Go consumer decoding old and new envelopes. Build deterministic Go evidence to protect declaration equivalence and route mutable changes through policy or migration; keep credentials and connection URLs out of output; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Topology Owner Contract contract for the supplied data.
